title: Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of 2025
short_title: Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill helps Alaska Native veterans who served in the Vietnam War get land they deserve. These veterans can receive up to 160 acres of federal land in Alaska, but they had to apply by December 29, 2025. The bill gives them five more years to apply, moving the deadline to December 29, 2030. This extra time helps make sure more eligible veterans and their families can get the land benefits they earned through their military service.
subjects: Wilderness and natural areas, wildlife refuges, wild rivers, habitats

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of two thousand and twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2  Extension of the Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the John D. Dingell, Jr. Conservation, Management, and Recreation Act. The change is simple. Where the old law said that Alaska Native Vietnam veterans had a five-year period to apply for land, this new law extends that time to a ten-year period. This gives these veterans more time to request land that they are entitled to receive.
